new_fix_this_run.description: Fixed record_outcome() to skip VETO rules. Root cause of the data corruption fixed in prior run (times_correct=25 for 4 applications): record_outcome() passed no hour_utc to matches(), causing night_session_block_v1 (conditions: hour_utc_min/max only) to match ALL closed trades and increment times_correct for every loss. On restart, this bug would re-corrupt times_correct immediately. Fix: VETO rules are now excluded from record_outcome() — their accuracy is tracked exclusively by counterfactual_learner.py which has the blocked-trade context. Only boost/penalize rules use record_outcome().
new_fix_this_run.impact: Prevents re-corruption of times_correct for veto rules on restart. Logically correct: veto rules track blocked trades (counterfactual), not closed trades (record_outcome). ZERO trading behavior change — accuracy tracking only.
new_fix_this_run.risk: ZERO — accuracy tracking fix only, no trading logic change
new_fix_this_run.root_cause_clarification: Prior run diagnosed the corruption as 'bulk historical initialization artifact'. Partially correct for rule_1777922205_0 (BTC trend boost). BUT for night_session_block_v1, the ONGOING bug in record_outcome() would have re-corrupted times_correct on every restart. The prior fix (reset to 0) was a band-aid; this run fixes the root cause.

code_changes_this_run.change: record_outcome() now skips VETO rules. Added explanatory comment: veto accuracy = counterfactual_learner (blocked-trade context), not trade-close callback. Prevents times_correct inflation from unrelated closed trades matching time-based or symbol/side conditions. Auto-retire logic for veto rules now depends solely on counterfactual_learner.py wiring (confirmed active at counterfactual_learner.py:459-471).
